_meta.ground_truth_notes: An attacker with shell on jsmith's laptop (or another corp host inside Building 7 wifi) hijacked jsmith's live ssh-agent socket and rode the live agent through to bastion-01. The smoking gun is the parent-PID lineage on the inbound sshd session (originated from a non-jsmith user shell on the corp wifi gateway segment) plus the agent-forwarding flag set by an unexpected upstream hop. The recent-ticket cover (INC-8821) is unrelated; the closure timestamp is post-hoc cover. Must-check tool: ssh_audit on bastion-01 for parent-PID lineage and AgentForwarding=yes from the new src_ip.

ssh_audit:host=bastion-01.corp|user=jsmi.anomaly_note: the 03:47 session is the FIRST agent-forwarded session for jsmith on bastion-01 in 180d; the upstream parent claims to be from 10.42.7.4 (bld7-wifi-gw) but no login session for jsmith exists on 10.42.7.4 in the same window — agent socket was used by a different uid

ssh_audit:host=bastion-01.corp|user=jsmi.note: see also the time-bounded query for parent-PID lineage and agent-forwarding-first-seen flag

ssh_audit:host=10.42.7.4|user=jsmith|tim.note: no jsmith login on bld7-wifi-gw (10.42.7.4) in 24h — the host the agent-forwarding chain claims to originate from

sessions.agent_forwarding_received_from_socket: /tmp/ssh-XXXX/agent.4422 (owned by uid=jsmith via shared kerberos pam socket — UNUSUAL)

ssh_audit:host=10.42.7.4|time_range=24h.anomaly_note: guest-wifi-shared uid logged in from 10.42.7.183 at 03:46:51 — 21 seconds before the bastion login. The agent socket on bld7-wifi-gw appears to be reachable to non-jsmith uids due to a permissive pam_ssh_agent_socket override on this host.

badge_log:user=jsmith|time_range=24h.note: jsmith badged OUT at 18:23 the previous evening; no badge IN for 2026-05-05 — physically not in Building 7 at 03:47. The src_ip is corp wifi but the badge claims jsmith is offsite.

dhcp_log:ip=10.42.7.183|time_range=12h.anomaly_note: 10.42.7.183 is currently leased to a Building 7 GUEST-wifi MAC — not a jsmith-issued corp asset. jsmith's normal device(s) historically live on 10.42.5.0/24.

vpn_log:user=jsmith|time_range=14d.note: jsmith VPN shows infrequent off-corp use; no active VPN session on 2026-05-05. Consistent with badge — jsmith was offsite without VPN.

ticket_get:id=INC-8821.title: rotate bastion-01 sshd config (key allowlist refresh)

comments.author: jsmith
comments.text: scheduled rotation, will close after deploy

ticket_get:id=INC-8821.anomaly_note: the closing comment was authored from 10.42.7.183 (the same guest-wifi IP as the bastion login). No linked CR/ticket-system change record. The closure looks like cover-text rather than the result of a deploy pipeline action — no commit, no helm/ansible run, no sshd_config diff attached.

tickets.title: rotate bastion-01 sshd config (key allowlist refresh)

siem_query:index=auth-log|filter=user=js.anomaly_note: 21-second gap between guest-wifi-shared login on the wifi gateway and jsmith publickey login on bastion-01 from the same client IP — consistent with an attacker pivoting through the wifi gateway's pam socket to jsmith's live agent. The subsequent hop to internal-prod-01 from bastion-01 also carries agent-forwarding.

siem_query:index=corp-wifi|filter=client.anomaly_note: MAC f4:30:b9:21:cc:14 is a first-time-seen device on corp-guest wifi (3 hours before the bastion login), captive-portal-registered with a partner-vendor email.

ssh_key_audit:fingerprint=SHA256:a1b2c3..note: key was NOT extracted from disk — it remains in jsmith's laptop ssh-agent. The bld7-wifi-gw entry indicates the agent socket was reachable through the shared pam configuration; this is the pivot point.

git_log:repo=infra-ansible|author=jsmith.note: no commits to infra-ansible / sshd-config-related repos in 14d — the INC-8821 'rotation' has no corresponding change-management artifact

slack_history:user=jsmith|time_range=24h.note: no slack activity from jsmith between 18:00 and 03:47; no mention of bastion rotation work in #infra or #sre

process_audit:host=bastion-01.corp|sessi.note: session reads sshd_config (recon), wipes shell history, then pivots agent-forwarded into internal-prod-01. Not consistent with 'rotate sshd config' which would write to it, not just read.
